title: Presigned Downloads, CloudFront Signatures, Cookies, and Revocation · Systems Transfer
summary: An independent team receives different tenants, file sizes, media types, regions, compliance duties, traffic, failures, and cost limits and transfers presigned downloads, cloudfront signatures, cookies, and revocation into a new time-bounded signed-delivery decision record. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of time-bounded signed-delivery decision record with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case pract…
objectives: Presign the exact version, response metadata, method, region, credential scope, and shortest useful expiry while treating the URL as a bearer secret.
Choose signed URL or signed cookie by object count, path scope, player behavior, client constraints, and leakage surface.
Choose canned or custom CloudFront policy and constrain expiry, optional start time, resource wildcard, and IP range without accidental overbreadth.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Commit to the mission model · Lab S3 Presigned Download
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab Go file-delivery team handles case 5972 during a cost-anomaly investigation. Build deterministic Go evidence to bound signed access by time, resource, key group, and explicit SHA-256 review; keep browser execution deterministic and move AWS claims to an authorized transfer gate; then double concurrent transfers and keep process resource limits intact. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Presign the exact version, response metadata, method, region, credential scope, and shortest useful expiry while treating the URL as a bearer secret.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: S3, CloudFront, and Go Commit to the mission model · Lab S3 Presigned Download.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for presign the exact version, response metadata, method, region, credential scope, and shortest useful expiry while treating the url as a bearer secret. For S3, CloudFront, and Go Commit to the mission model · Lab S3 Presigned Download, keep S3 Presigned Download connected to executable Go storage and delivery decision evidence.

Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Presign the exact version, response metadata, method, region, credential scope, and shortest useful expiry while treating the URL as a bearer secret.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Signed Access Revocation · changed case · Lab Signed Access Revocation
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab Go file-delivery team handles case 8305 during a compliance restore drill. Build deterministic Go evidence to bound signed access by time, resource, key group, and explicit SHA-256 review; record cleanup, rollback, restoration, and residual-risk ownership; then cancel halfway through and prove every body, part, and goroutine reaches a terminal state. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Model expiry-at-request-start, range continuation, key-group rotation, authorization changes, logs and referrers, cache behavior, and emergency revocation limits.
